title: Animal Feed Shortage Threatens Meat and Dairy Supply
detail: Iranian livestock farmers warn of an imminent collapse in meat and dairy production as imported animal feed becomes unaffordable following the elimination of the preferential exchange rate. Customs data shows feed imports have fallen sharply since early December. Poultry farmers in Isfahan and Khorasan provinces report culling flocks they can no longer afford to feed. The Ministry of Agriculture acknowledges the crisis but offers no timeline for relief, as the government lacks the foreign currency to subsidize agricultural imports.

media.caption: Animal feed shortage threatens Iran's meat and dairy supply as import costs triple

title: Criticism Mounts Over IRGC's Economic Dominance
detail: Euronews publishes an in-depth investigation into the IRGC's economic empire, documenting how the Revolutionary Guard controls vast swaths of Iran's economy including construction, telecommunications, oil, and banking sectors. The report details how wealth and opportunity are concentrated among senior officials, influential clerics, and IRGC commanders while ordinary Iranians face economic ruin. The piece fuels existing public anger at regime priorities, with social media users sharing the article despite internet throttling.

media.caption: Investigation reveals IRGC controls vast swaths of Iran's economy while citizens face ruin

title: Localized Protests Simmer in Kurdish and Arab Provinces
detail: Small but persistent protests continue in Kermanshah, Sanandaj, and Ahvaz as economic conditions deteriorate further. Demonstrators in Kurdish-majority areas chant against both economic mismanagement and ethnic discrimination in resource allocation. In oil-rich Khuzestan, Arab communities point out the bitter irony of living in poverty atop Iran's largest hydrocarbon reserves. Security forces maintain heavy presence but avoid large-scale confrontation, suggesting the regime is wary of triggering broader unrest before the holidays.

media.caption: Localized protests simmer in Kurdish and Arab provinces over economic crisis and ethnic discrimination
